column1: # Histone H4 — one of the most conserved proteins known.
# H4 packages DNA in every eukaryote and tolerates almost no change: across
# ~1.5 billion years (mammal, bird, insect, nematode, fungus, ciliate, plant)
# the ~103-residue sequence differs at only a handful of positions. With
# relativeTo=Human nearly the whole alignment renders as dots, and the rare
# substitutions (mostly in the divergent ciliate/plant/yeast rows) are the
# entire story — the opposite extreme from a fast-evolving protein.
# Columns: UniProt accession <TAB> row label. Human first (reference).
